---
# Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one
# or more contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file
# distributed with this work for additional information
# regarding copyright ownership.  The ASF licenses this file
# to you under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the
# "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance
# with the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#   http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing,
# software distributed under the License is distributed on an
# "AS IS" BASIS, WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY
# KIND, either express or implied.  See the License for the
# specific language governing permissions and limitations
# under the License.

title: ST_ReducePrecision
description: Reduces the coordinate precision of a geometry or geography to the specified grid size.
kernels:
  - returns: geometry
    args:
    - geometry
    - name: grid_size
      type: double
  - returns: geography
    args:
    - geography
    - name: grid_size
      type: double
---

## Description

Reduces the precision of coordinates in a geometry or geography by snapping them to a grid of the specified size. This can be useful for reducing data size and ensuring coordinates align to a consistent grid.

The `grid_size` parameter specifies the size of the grid cells. Coordinates are rounded to the nearest grid intersection. For example, a grid size of 0.001 would round coordinates to 3 decimal places.

Z and M values are preserved but not affected by the grid snapping.

## Examples

### Geometry

```sql
SELECT ST_AsText(ST_ReducePrecision(
    ST_GeomFromText('POINT (1.123456 2.654321)'),
    0.01
));
-- Returns: POINT(1.12 2.65)
```

```sql
SELECT ST_AsText(ST_ReducePrecision(
    ST_GeomFromText('LINESTRING (0.123 0.456, 1.789 2.012)'),
    0.1
));
-- Returns: LINESTRING(0.1 0.5,1.8 2)
```

### Geography

```sql
SELECT ST_AsText(ST_ReducePrecision(
    ST_GeogFromWKT('POINT (1.123456 2.654321)'),
    0.001
));
-- Returns: POINT(1.123 2.654)
```
